_readme: This file drives the whole concept page — edit text/images here, no code needed. Field guide with examples: content/concepts/README.md. View at /concept/?id=lunar-base

live_view.heading: Live View
live_view.note: [Live placeholder scene — drag to orbit, right-drag to move, scroll to zoom; pins, framing and copy are samples]

_camera_generated.note: envelope fields generated from the capture rig — do not hand-edit look_at_m/distance min,max/angles; edit the vantage in Blender and re-export

pins.title: Crew EVA
pins.text: [sample] Two crew on surface operations near the habitat approach path. Final hotspot copy lands with the scene bible.

pins.title: Surface rover
pins.text: [sample] Unpressurized utility rover staged for a logistics run. Placeholder position — authored per scene.

pins.title: Boulder field
pins.text: [sample] Ejecta boulders shield the northern perimeter. Placeholder position — authored per scene.

overview.heading: Overview
overview.intro: [sample copy — content in production] A crewed surface habitat cluster near the lunar south pole, built out in the years following Artemis IV. [sample copy — the final summary will follow the scene bible and cover habitat modules, power, and surface logistics.]

features.label: F_01 — Site
features.title: South-polar ridge
features.text: [sample — an elevated ridge site trades the 29.5-day lunar day-night cycle for near-continuous low-angle sunlight, with permanently shadowed craters in rover range]

features.label: F_02 — Surface Ops
features.title: Mobility & logistics
features.text: [sample — regolith dust is electrostatically charged and abrasive; airlock and radiator placement in the scene reflects dust-mitigation spacing]

features.label: F_03 — Comms
features.title: Orbital relay
features.text: [sample — one-way light time to Earth is ~1.3 s; a relay covers far-side and shadowed-region operations in near-real time]

features.label: F_04 — Shielding
features.title: Regolith overburden
features.text: [sample — a few meters of regolith over the habitat modules attenuates galactic cosmic radiation for long-duration stays]

blocks.text: [sample copy — this section is a free-form editorial area: write blog-style chapters of any length here, with headings, paragraphs and images between chapters as needed. The template renders whatever this file provides.]

[sample copy — a second paragraph demonstrating running text rhythm. The pressurized modules arrive as integrated lander payloads and are connected by short tunnels; the regolith-shielded core hosts crew quarters and the radiation shelter.]

[sample copy — solar arrays track the low polar sun on short masts; a fission surface power unit a kilometre up-slope provides night and contingency power. Radiators run cool on the permanently shaded side of the berm.]

[sample copy — cargo landers arrive at ~6-month intervals; unpressurized rovers move payloads from the landing zone along a dust-managed route. The scene shows the route markers and the berm crossing.]
